Test Scenario: Verify login with valid credentials and TFA
Objective: Ensure user can log in successfully with TFA
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid username and password; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter valid OTP sent to registered contact; 5. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): User is redirected to dashboard after OTP verification

Test Scenario: Verify login with invalid OTP
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid username and password; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter invalid OTP; 5. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): Error message 'Invalid OTP' shown; access denied

Test Scenario: Verify login with blank OTP field
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid username and password; 3. Click Login; 4. Leave OTP field blank; 5. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): Error message 'OTP required' shown

Test Scenario: Verify login with alphabetic/special characters in OTP field
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid username and password; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter alphabetic/special characters in OTP field; 5. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): Error message 'OTP must be numeric' shown

Test Scenario: Verify OTP expiration handling
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid username and password; 3. Click Login; 4. Wait for OTP to expire; 5. Enter expired OTP; 6. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): Error message 'OTP expired' shown; user prompted to request new OTP

Test Scenario: Verify multiple login attempts require TFA each time
Objective: Ensure TFA triggers for every login
Test Steps: 1. Login with valid credentials and OTP; 2. Logout; 3. Login again with valid credentials; 4. Enter OTP
Expected Result (ER): TFA is required for each login; access granted after OTP verification

Test Scenario: Verify alternate flow for OTP request retry
Objective: Ensure new OTP can be requested before expiration
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Click 'Resend OTP' before expiration; 5. Enter new OTP
Expected Result (ER): New OTP is generated, previous OTP invalidated; access granted after new OTP verification

Test Scenario: Verify multiple invalid OTP attempts lock account or prompt alternative authentication
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter invalid OTP 3+ times
Expected Result (ER): Account is temporarily locked or alternative authentication prompted

Test Scenario: Verify OTP length boundaries (min/max)
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter OTP shorter/longer than required; 5. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): Error message for incorrect OTP length shown

Test Scenario: Verify OTP with leading/trailing whitespace
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter OTP with spaces; 5. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): Whitespace is stripped; OTP validated correctly

Test Scenario: Verify session established after valid OTP
Test Steps: 1. Complete login and OTP verification
Expected Result (ER): Session tokens generated; user granted access

Test Scenario: Verify TFA applies to all user types
Test Steps: 1. Login as each user type; 2. Complete TFA
Expected Result (ER): TFA required for all user types

Test Scenario: Verify system clock out of sync handling
Objective: Ensure OTP validation with clock sync
Pre-requisites: User account exists; system clock misaligned
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter OTP; 5. Click Submit
Expected Result (ER): System resynchronizes or rejects OTP with guidance

Test Scenario: Verify OTP delivered successfully via SMS/email
Pre-requisites: User account exists with valid contact
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login
Expected Result (ER): OTP delivered within 10 seconds to registered contact

Test Scenario: Verify OTP not received due to network failure
Objective: Ensure error handling for delivery failure
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Simulate network failure
Expected Result (ER): Error message shown; user can retry OTP delivery

Test Scenario: Verify multiple OTP requests (sequential/rapid)
Objective: Ensure rate limiting and unique OTPs
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Click 'Resend OTP' multiple times
Expected Result (ER): New OTP generated each time; previous OTPs invalidated; rate limiting applied

Test Scenario: Verify OTP duplicate prevention
Objective: Ensure only one unique OTP sent
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Click 'Resend OTP' rapidly
Expected Result (ER): Only one unique OTP sent; duplicate requests handled

Test Scenario: Verify system load handling for OTP delivery
Test Steps: 1. Simulate 100+ users logging in simultaneously
Expected Result (ER): All OTP deliveries processed without delay

Test Scenario: Verify OTP delivery logging (success/failure)
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Complete OTP delivery (success/failure)
Expected Result (ER): System logs delivery status with timestamp, user ID, method

Test Scenario: Verify OTP service provider failover
Pre-requisites: User account exists; primary provider down
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login
Expected Result (ER): OTP delivered via secondary provider

Test Scenario: Verify OTP format validation (numeric, length)
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login
Expected Result (ER): OTP is numeric and correct length

Test Scenario: Verify 'Resend OTP' button visibility
Objective: Ensure user can request new OTP
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Wait without entering OTP
Expected Result (ER): 'Resend OTP' button visible; user can request new OTP

Test Scenario: Verify OTP expiry just before/after limit
Test Steps: 1. Open login page; 2. Enter valid credentials; 3. Click Login; 4. Enter OTP at 9:59 and after 10:00
Expected Result (ER): OTP accepted just before expiry; rejected just after

Test Scenario: Verify single tab login best practice
Objective: Ensure login works with single tab
Test Steps: 1. Open multiple tabs; 2. Close all but one; 3. Login in single tab
Expected Result (ER): Login completes; OTP delivered; session established

Test Scenario: Verify tab duplication after login maintains session
Objective: Ensure session persists across duplicated tabs
Test Steps: 1. Login in one tab; 2. Duplicate tab
Expected Result (ER): Session maintained; no re-authentication required

Test Scenario: Verify session token sharing across duplicated tabs
Test Steps: 1. Login in one tab; 2. Duplicate tab; 3. Navigate in both tabs
Expected Result (ER): Session token shared; state synchronized

Test Scenario: Verify logout impact on duplicated tabs
Test Steps: 1. Login in multiple duplicated tabs; 2. Logout in one tab
Expected Result (ER): All tabs logout or only current tab logs out (per design)

Test Scenario: Verify multiple simultaneous login attempts (same account)
Test Steps: 1. Open multiple tabs; 2. Attempt login in each tab simultaneously
Expected Result (ER): System handles gracefully; clear guidance or error shown

Test Scenario: Verify OTP delivery with multiple pending login attempts
Test Steps: 1. Open 3+ tabs; 2. Attempt login in each tab simultaneously
Expected Result (ER): OTP delivered correctly; only one valid session created

Test Scenario: Verify session inconsistency detection
Test Steps: 1. Login in duplicated tabs; 2. Modify session state in one tab; 3. Perform action in other tab
Expected Result (ER): System maintains consistency or warns user

Test Scenario: Verify tab closure and session persistence
Test Steps: 1. Login in multiple tabs; 2. Close some tabs; 3. Continue in remaining tabs
Expected Result (ER): Session remains active in open tabs

Test Scenario: Verify cross-tab navigation
Objective: Ensure session is consistent across tabs
Test Steps: 1. Login in multiple tabs; 2. Switch between tabs
Expected Result (ER): Session remains active; data is current

Test Scenario: Verify browser refresh on duplicated tab
Test Steps: 1. Login in duplicated tabs; 2. Refresh one tab
Expected Result (ER): Session maintained; no re-authentication required

Test Scenario: Verify new tab opening after login
Objective: Ensure session persists in new tab
Test Steps: 1. Login in one tab; 2. Open new tab and navigate to app
Expected Result (ER): Session maintained or re-authentication required (per design)

Test Scenario: Verify private/incognito tab session isolation
Test Steps: 1. Login in regular tab; 2. Open incognito tab; 3. Access app
Expected Result (ER): Incognito tab requires separate authentication

Test Scenario: Verify multiple user accounts in different tabs
Pre-requisites: User A and User B accounts exist
Test Steps: 1. Login as User A in one tab; 2. Login as User B in another tab
Expected Result (ER): System prevents simultaneous sessions or manages separately

Test Scenario: Verify file upload across tabs
Test Steps: 1. Login in multiple tabs; 2. Upload file in one tab; 3. Access app in other tab
Expected Result (ER): Uploads processed independently; session maintained

Test Scenario: Verify session timeout on one tab
Test Steps: 1. Login in multiple tabs; 2. Wait for timeout in one tab; 3. Switch to other tab
Expected Result (ER): System expands timeout or requires re-login (per design)

Test Scenario: Verify back button navigation after login
Test Steps: 1. Login in one tab; 2. Click back button multiple times in duplicated tab
Expected Result (ER): Navigation to login page prevented; session state maintained

Test Scenario: Verify popup/modal behavior across tabs
Test Steps: 1. Login in multiple tabs; 2. Trigger modal in one tab; 3. Switch between tabs
Expected Result (ER): Modal state preserved or handled; session continues

Test Scenario: Verify session conflict when one tab logs out user globally
Test Steps: 1. Login in multiple tabs; 2. Logout in one tab; 3. Access protected resources in other tabs
Expected Result (ER): Remaining tabs show logout confirmation or redirect to login

Test Scenario: Verify TFA frequency configuration (every login/daily)
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency to 'every login'; 2. Login/logout multiple times
Expected Result (ER): TFA required for every login

Test Scenario: Verify TFA frequency configuration (daily mode)
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency to 'daily'; 2. Login multiple times same day
Expected Result (ER): TFA required only once per day

Test Scenario: Verify TFA reset at midnight (daily mode)
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency to 'daily'; 2. Login before and after midnight
Expected Result (ER): TFA required again after midnight

Test Scenario: Verify configuration change by admin (every to daily)
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency to 'every login'; 2. Change to 'daily'; 3. Login
Expected Result (ER): New logins follow 'daily' rule; existing sessions unaffected

Test Scenario: Verify configuration change by admin (daily to every)
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency to 'daily'; 2. Change to 'every login'; 3. Login
Expected Result (ER): New logins follow 'every login' rule

Test Scenario: Verify configuration audit logging
Test Steps: 1. Change TFA frequency; 2. Check audit log
Expected Result (ER): Audit log shows timestamp, admin ID, previous and new setting

Test Scenario: Verify configuration takes effect immediately for new sessions
Objective: Ensure new sessions respect new frequency
Test Steps: 1. Change TFA frequency; 2. Login with new session
Expected Result (ER): New sessions follow new frequency; existing sessions unaffected

Test Scenario: Verify concurrent configuration changes handled gracefully
Test Steps: 1. Two admins change TFA frequency simultaneously
Expected Result (ER): System resolves conflict; last-write-wins or locking applied

Test Scenario: Verify invalid configuration value is rejected
Test Steps: 1. Attempt to set unsupported value (e.g., 'weekly')
Expected Result (ER): System rejects change; error shown; valid options presented

Test Scenario: Verify configuration changed during active sessions
Objective: Ensure active sessions continue under old rule
Pre-requisites: Admin access to system settings; users logged in
Test Steps: 1. Change TFA frequency; 2. Active users continue; new logins follow new rule
Expected Result (ER): Active sessions continue; new logins follow new rule

Test Scenario: Verify timezone handling for daily mode
Objective: Ensure daily reset applied per timezone
Pre-requisites: Admin access to system settings; users in multiple timezones
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency to 'daily'; 2. Login around midnight local time
Expected Result (ER): Daily reset applied per user's timezone

Test Scenario: Verify daylight saving time handling for daily mode
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency to 'daily'; 2. Login during DST transition
Expected Result (ER): TFA prompt not duplicated or skipped

Test Scenario: Verify default configuration on fresh install
Test Steps: 1. Initialize system; 2. Check TFA frequency
Expected Result (ER): Default is 'every login'

Test Scenario: Verify configuration persistence after system restart
Test Steps: 1. Set TFA frequency; 2. Restart system; 3. Check TFA frequency
Expected Result (ER): Configuration persists as previously set

Test Scenario: Verify rollback of configuration change
Test Steps: 1. Change TFA frequency; 2. Rollback to previous setting; 3. Login
Expected Result (ER): Previous rule restored; new logins follow restored rule

Test Scenario: Verify configuration visibility to admins
Test Steps: 1. Navigate to TFA configuration section
Expected Result (ER): Current setting displayed; options to change

Test Scenario: Verify report generation for TFA frequency changes
Test Steps: 1. Generate report of configuration changes
Expected Result (ER): Report shows all changes with timestamps and admin info

Test Scenario: Verify performance impact of frequent configuration changes
Objective: Ensure system handles rapid changes efficiently
Test Steps: 1. Change TFA frequency multiple times rapidly
Expected Result (ER): System processes changes efficiently; no degradation
